summary: Round-2 re-review of internal/pretty tests for task-01. All five round-1 findings (2 major + 3 minor) are fully addressed and verified against the implementation; go test ./internal/pretty/... is green (TestSize, TestCeil, TestReserveWidth, TestRateUnit, TestRateUnit_boundary, TestRateUnit_property all PASS). (1) The documented widen-beyond-reserve path is now pinned by explicit table cases: RateUnit(10239000, disk, 4)="10000GB/s" (Ceil(10239000/1024)=Ceil(9999.02)=10000, single promotion) and RateUnit(10000000, net, 4)="10000Gbps" (Ceil(1e7/1000)=10000) — both confirm the unit is promoted exactly once and the field widens rather than truncating. (2) TestRateUnit_property is now honest: it walks to 1e9 (entering the widen region for both families), keeps the universal never-narrower-than-reserve invariant, asserts digits equal the never-truncated expected integer (Ceil(v) in-reserve / Ceil(v/divisor) once promoted), and splits the width assertion into two truthful regimes — in-reserve = exactly width, widen = exactly digit-wide. The previously false universal 'always exactly reserve width' claim is gone, and the comment scopes both regimes. (3) ReserveWidth now has width-4 cases {9999->"9999"} and {10000->"10000"} covering the speeds/err/coll reserve. (4) Default/empty-family fallback is exercised: RateUnit(50,"",4)="  50MB/s". (5) The TestRateUnit comment now states the precise overflow rule (Ceil(v) > 10^width-1). Every test still passes the litmus check: removing the Ceil division/promotion in pretty.go would break the boundary and widen assertions; removing the %*d padding would break ReserveWidth and the in-reserve width assertion. No regressions, no new findings.
